feat: Add Foundry evaluator-optimizer integration (experimental) (#384)

## Summary

Adds Azure Foundry agent evaluator integration for LangGraph,
implementing the [evaluator-optimizer
pattern](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/workflows-agents#evaluator-optimizer)
with real [Foundry builtin
evaluators](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/concepts/evaluation-evaluators/agent-evaluators).
All new APIs are marked `@experimental()`.

## Library: `langchain_azure_ai.evaluation` (experimental)

New evaluation module with Foundry evaluator wrappers:

- **`FoundryEvaluator`** — wraps Foundry builtin evaluators, creates
eval definitions, runs them, polls for results, and parses outputs
- **`FoundryEvaluatorSuite`** — runs multiple evaluators against the
same input, tracks last results via `all_passed`, and now exposes
`close()` to propagate cached-client cleanup
- **`messages_to_foundry_format()`** — converts LangChain messages to
Foundry/OpenAI-style query/response payloads
- **`tool_schemas_to_foundry_format()`** — converts LangChain tool
objects to Foundry tool definitions
- **`create_eval_optimize_subgraph()`** — reusable LangGraph builder for
evaluate → conditional refine loops
- **`create_analyst_subgraph()`** — analyst-oriented helper now
parameterized via transformation callbacks instead of sample-specific
imports/state assumptions

## Tracer integration

- Adds `gen_ai.evaluation.*` semantic convention constants to the tracer
`Attrs` surface
- Adds `emit_evaluation_event()` to `AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer` for
`gen_ai.evaluation.result` events attached to the evaluated
`invoke_agent` span

## Review-driven refinements

- Removed sample-specific `importlib.import_module("eval_config")`
coupling from library code
- Enforced iteration limits in graph state rather than a shared closure
counter, making eval-optimize loops per-invocation and concurrency-safe
- Added reusable `state_to_eval_input` / `eval_output_to_state`
callbacks so analyst helpers can adapt caller-specific state schemas
- Hardened section/state access so dict/object-shaped inputs are handled
safely
- Cached/reused Foundry clients with locking and added `close()`
lifecycle support
- `FoundryEvaluatorSuite.close()` now propagates evaluator cleanup so
suite users can release cached project/OpenAI clients
- `_get_client()` now takes the client lock even when returning cached
clients, avoiding races with `close()`, and its implementation/docstring
were simplified to match the actual control flow
- Switched polling to `time.monotonic()` for accurate timeout tracking
- Timed-out eval runs now return explicit failed `FoundryEvalResult`
values with timeout explanations
- Normalized label -> passed handling consistently across response
parsing branches
- Removed duplicate unused evaluation constants from the legacy
`_semantic_conventions_gen_ai.py` module so the tracer has a single
canonical attribute definition surface

## Tests

Expanded unit coverage now validates:
- Message/tool conversion helpers
- Foundry evaluator wrappers and timeout behavior
- Client reuse behavior and locked cached-client reads
- Suite-level cleanup via `FoundryEvaluatorSuite.close()`
- Consistent result parsing across API shapes
- Tracer `emit_evaluation_event()` output
- Evaluator-optimizer helpers, concurrent iteration limiting, custom
state mapping, and safe section handling

Targeted validation currently passes (`test_evaluation.py`, ruff, mypy
on changed files).

commit.message:
Refactor get_storage_path* functions (#12906)

We were using Project.get_storage_path, which required to manually pass
attributes from a version. Most of the time we already have a version
object in place, so I "moved" the method to the Version class. The old
method was also used to get file of a downloadable media type, I moved
this to another method. Version.get_storage_path also accepts a
filename, so we don't need to join the paths manually everytime we want
to get the storage path from a file.

We also were setting a version_type attribute to force all storage calls
to be external/internal, but we should make sure the version we are
getting the file from matches the external domain it's served from, this
check is already done by the unresolver. We also have tests for this
already.

Project.get_storage_paths wasn't returning the paths used by PR
previews, we were missing deleting those from storage when the project
was deleted.

Ref https://github.com/readthedocs/readthedocs.org/issues/12899
Closes https://github.com/readthedocs/readthedocs.org/issues/10220

commit.message:
fix: strip transport prefix before prepending containers-storage: (#11)

skopeoExportOCI and CheckImage both prepend their own transport prefix
(containers-storage: / docker://) to the image reference. When the
caller passes an image that already carries a containers-storage: prefix
(as the tuna-installer live-ISO path does), this produces a double prefix
that causes skopeo to fail:

  containers-storage:containers-storage:ghcr.io/org/image:tag

Add bareImageRef() which strips any OCI transport prefix
("scheme://ref" or "scheme:ref" style) before the transport is added
back by the caller. Apply it in skopeoExportOCI (line 443) and both
SkopeoInspectFn calls in CheckImage (lines 612/615).

commit.message:
perf: optimize property load IC and generic arithmetic fast paths

- Add jit_runtime_lda_named_property_r15 entry point that accepts
  pre-cached RtPtrs cell pointer, eliminating one TLS lookup per
  property load when R15 carries the pointer.

- Extract lda_named_property_inner_with_ptrs to share the inner
  implementation between the TLS and R15 variants.

- Use get_by_offset_unchecked in IC hit paths (both lda_named and
  inline_lda_named) since shape_id match guarantees offset validity,
  saving a bounds check per property load.

- Add get_rt_string_constant_ref_with_ptrs to eliminate redundant
  RT_PTRS TLS lookups on IC miss paths when ptrs are already cached.

- Replace 6-comparison Smi range checks in generic_add/sub/mul with
  is_smi() (sign-extension check) + checked_add/sub/mul (single
  overflow-flag test) for a tighter fast-path code sequence.

- Mark arithmetic slow paths (HeapNumber fallback) as #[cold] to
  improve code layout for the Smi fast path.

- Apply is_smi() helper consistently to bitwise_and, bitwise_or,
  negate, increment, and decrement stubs.

fix: suppress interactive apt/needrestart prompts during provisioning

cloud-init runs without a TTY, so any interactive dialog (needrestart,
debconf whiptail) fails with 'Failed to open terminal' and pollutes the
provision log with ANSI/VT100 escape sequences.

Add to all provisioning scripts (bootstrap.sh, setup.sh, proxy.sh,
agents.sh) and the configurator generator template:

  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  export NEEDRESTART_MODE=a

DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive prevents debconf from launching
interactive frontends. NEEDRESTART_MODE=a makes needrestart
automatically restart services without prompting.

commit.message:
fix(proxy): re-apply chown after caddy validate to fix log permission

caddy validate runs as root and can create /var/log/caddy/access.log
owned by root before Caddy's systemd service (running as caddy user)
gets a chance to write to it.

Moving the chown to after validate (and before systemctl restart)
ensures the caddy user always owns the log file at startup.

Fixes recurring ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED on fresh provisioning.

commit.message:
Fix camera depth supervision: reference-based approach

Replace Gaussian-rendered depth (corrupted by 547m-scale Gaussians)
with a pre-computed GT-projected reference depth image.

Changes:
- Add _build_cam_depth_refs(): projects LiDAR pts at GT T_l2c,
  scatter-min rasterises to sparse depth image (H, W), one per frame
- Pre-compute all reference images before training loop when
  lambda_cam_depth > 0
- Replace render_camera_raytracing() block with reference lookup:
  bilinear-sample d_ref at projected pixel coords; gradient flows
  only through d_lidar(T_l2c) → T_l2c, not through Gaussians
- Update viz_cam_depth_supervision.py to use the same reference
  approach: Panel 2 now shows GT-projected reference depth instead
  of Gaussian-rendered depth; remove gaussians dependency from
  visualise_frame()

Root cause addressed:
  LiDAR-trained Gaussians have max scale=547m; these huge Gaussians
  act as infinite planes blocking all camera rays (~26m depth instead
  of expected ~15m). The new approach bypasses Gaussian rendering
  entirely for the camera depth supervision reference.

Also removes render_camera_raytracing import from viz script.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix private method access on proxy objects: bypass proxy traps for private keys in GetMethod

When a class constructor returns a Proxy wrapping 'this', private fields
and methods are installed on the proxy object (not the target). GetMethod
was calling read_named_property which triggers the proxy's get trap,
causing the trap to look for private keys on the target (which doesn't
have them). Per spec, private name resolution should never go through
[[Get]]/proxy traps. Now GetMethod reads directly from the object's map
for private keys, matching GetProperty's existing behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[KBM] Manual key selection — code review fixes (#46377)

Addresses code review feedback on the KBM manual key selection feature.
No new user-facing behavior; all changes are correctness, robustness,
and maintainability fixes.

## Summary of the Pull Request

- **Localization:** All hard-coded `RemappingDialog.Title` assignments
replaced with `ResourceHelper.GetString()`; added
`RemappingDialog_TitleEdit` resource key
- **VK_DISABLED centralization:** Replaced scattered `0x100`/`"256"`
literals and local `const string vkDisabledCode` with `private const int
VkDisabled = 0x100` / `private const string VkDisabledString = "256"` on
`MainPage`
- **Disable action validation:** Added
`ValidationHelper.ValidateDisableMapping()` — same trigger-key rules as
other action types (empty keys, modifier-only, illegal shortcuts,
duplicates, conflicting modifier variants); wired into
`ValidateMapping()` switch
- **Binding-safe dropdown revert:** `TriggerKeyDropDown_KeyChanged` /
`ActionKeyDropDown_KeyChanged` no longer set `dropDown.KeyName =
e.OldKeyName` on failure (breaks `{Binding}` expression); now use
`RevertKeySelection(keys, index)` which does
`ObservableCollection.RemoveAt` + `Insert` to force a binding-tracked
refresh without touching the DP directly. `NewKeyCode == 0` ("None") is
rejected via the same path
- **Dropdown validation:** `ValidateDropDownSelection` skips
`string.IsNullOrEmpty` placeholder slots (added by
`HandleAutoGrowShrink`) when checking repeated-modifier and max-size
rules
- **`SetActionType`:** Replaced hard-coded `SelectedIndex` with
tag-matching iteration over `ActionTypeComboBox.Items`; immune to XAML
item reorder
- **`ServiceStatusHelper`:** Dispose all `Process` objects returned by
`GetProcessesByName` before returning; prevents handle accumulation on
the 3-second polling timer
- **`KeyDropDownButton.GetKeyList()`:** Filter out `KeyCode == 0`
entries (native "None" sentinel for shortcut lists) before caching
- **`SettingsManager`:** `_mappingService!` used consistently in
`CreateSettingsFromKeyboardManagerService`
- **`KeyboardHookHelper`:** Constructor catch broadened from
`DllNotFoundException or InvalidOperationException` to `Exception`

## PR Checklist

- [ ] **Communication:** I've discussed this with core contributors
already. If the work hasn't been agreed, this work might be rejected
- [ ] **Tests:** Added/updated and all pass
- [x] **Localization:** All end-user-facing strings can be localized
- [ ] **Dev docs:** Added/updated
- [ ] **New binaries:** Added on the required places
- [ ] [JSON for
signing](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/.pipelines/ESRPSigning_core.json)
for new binaries
- [ ] [WXS for
installer](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/installer/PowerToysSetup/Product.wxs)
for new binaries and localization folder
- [ ] [YML for CI
pipeline](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/.pipelines/ci/templates/build-powertoys-steps.yml)
for new test projects
- [ ] [YML for signed
pipeline](https://github.com/microsoft/PowerToys/blob/main/.pipelines/release.yml)
- [ ] **Documentation updated:** If checked, please file a pull request
on [our docs
repo](https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/windows-uwp/tree/docs/hub/powertoys)
and link it here: #xxx

## Detailed Description of the Pull Request / Additional comments

The `RevertKeySelection` pattern: in WinUI, assigning directly to a
bound `DependencyProperty` overwrites the binding expression. Using
`ObservableCollection.RemoveAt` + `Insert` instead raises
`CollectionChanged(Replace)`, causing the binding to re-read from the
source without clearing the expression.

```csharp
private static void RevertKeySelection(ObservableCollection<string> keys, int index)
{
    string current = keys[index];
    keys.RemoveAt(index);
    keys.Insert(index, current);
}
```

## Validation Steps Performed

Manually verified: dropdown key selection and revert on invalid
selection, Disable mapping save/load with the new validation, "None"
absent from key picker flyout, `SetActionType` correctly selects items
with preserved XAML order.

commit.message:
perf(maglev): CreateMappedArguments trampoline + inline JIT-to-JIT closure dispatch

Two targeted changes to fix the closure_counter benchmark (21000x gap):

1. Route CreateMappedArguments/CreateUnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter
   through the runtime trampoline instead of the catch-all unconditional
   deopt.  All sloppy-mode functions emit CreateMappedArguments at entry;
   the old catch-all immediately deopted, preventing any sloppy function
   from ever executing Maglev code.

2. Inline JIT-to-JIT dispatch for monomorphic closure calls: on the cache
   hit path, the caller saves/restores BytecodeArray via R15->RtPtrs cell,
   allocates a register file on the x86 stack, and jumps directly to the
   callee entry point — zero Rust FFI calls on the hot path.

Also: RtPtrs gains repr(C) + RTPTRS_BYTECODE_OFFSET const for stable
field offsets in JIT assembly.

fix: resolve provisioning failures in cloud-init environment

Three bugs caused complete provisioning failure when run via cloud-init:

1. HOME not set: cloud-init runcmd runs without a login shell, so the
   HOME env var is unset. The Coder install script requires HOME to
   resolve cache/install paths.
   Fix: export HOME=\ in bootstrap.sh and setup.sh.

2. Wrong Coder binary path: setup.sh hardcoded ExecStart=/usr/bin/coder
   but Coder installs to /usr/local/bin/coder (--method=standalone).
   Fix: use /usr/local/bin/coder in the systemd unit.

3. Coder running as root: Coder's built-in PostgreSQL refuses to start
   as root. The systemd unit had no User= directive, defaulting to root.
   Fix: create a dedicated 'coder' system user and set User=coder/Group=coder.
   Also switched deprecated CODER_ADDRESS to CODER_HTTP_ADDRESS.

4. Caddy log permission: proxy.sh created /var/log/caddy as root, then
   Caddy (running as caddy user) couldn't write access.log.
   Fix: chown -R caddy:caddy /var/log/caddy after mkdir.

Closes #15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Four-layer defense against system-level PA sink mute going unnoticed:

1. UI warning: device card shows orange 'Sink muted' status with blocked

   assistant with 'Unmute speaker' primary action; new /api/unmute_sink
   endpoint for direct PA sink unmute bypassing MA routing

4. Auto-unmute safety net: parent-side watchdog auto-unmutes after 30s
   grace period if desync persists; daemon startup unmute retries up to

commit.message:
perf(maglev): optimize arithmetic loop codegen via relaxed Phi coalescing

Three optimizations targeting the arithmetic_loop_10k benchmark:

1. Relaxed Phi coalescing (regalloc.rs): When a Phi's live range extends
   past the back-edge input only due to exit-block uses, coalescing is
   still safe because exit blocks branch before the body modifies the
   register. Compute effective_phi_end within loop blocks only.

2. Accumulator chain extension (regalloc.rs): After coalescing, walk
   backward from the back-edge input through single-use binary arithmetic
   ops (Add/Sub/Mul), reassigning intermediates to the Phi's register.
   This lets the codegen emit in-place ALU updates (no MOV copies).

3. Int32 bitwise/shift ops as narrow consumers (codegen.rs): Add
   Int32BitwiseOr/And/Xor and Int32ShiftLeft/Right/RightLogical to
   is_narrow_int32_consumer, preventing unnecessary MOVSXD when these
   ops aren't eliminated by identity optimization.

4. Constant folding for bitwise ops (optimizer.rs): Add Int32BitwiseOr,
   Int32BitwiseAnd, Int32BitwiseXor to fold_block_constants so constant
   bitwise expressions are folded at compile time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: reorganize markdown files into proper structure (#4)

* fix(ar): improve drag drop position and trash zone deletion

Problem 1: Objects couldn't be dropped everywhere during drag
- Root cause: ARCore hit test only updates position on valid plane surfaces
- Fix: Keep last valid position when hit test fails, object stays in place
- Added lastValidDragPosition to track successful drag positions

Problem 2: Trash zone deletion not working reliably
- Root cause: onDragUpdate only called when currentObj found in placedObjects
- Fix: Always call onDragUpdate even if object not found (use default position)
- Added final trash zone check in onDragEnd to ensure deletion works

Fixes: fix-drag-drop-position, fix-trash-zone-delete
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ar): fix trash zone detection with proper X and Y coordinate check

- Added isOverTrashZone() helper function that checks both X (right side) and Y (bottom) coordinates
- Fixed trash zone hit detection to properly detect when object is dragged to bottom-right corner
- Updated onDragMove and onDragEnd to use the new isOverTrashZone() function
- Constants: TRASH_ZONE_SIZE_DP=80, TRASH_ZONE_MARGIN_DP=16

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: code review fixes and drag improvements

- Fixed magic strings in TrashZone (now uses localized strings)
- Removed unused 'progress' variable
- Added release_to_delete and drag_to_delete strings (EN and TR)
- Improved drag movement with camera ray projection fallback
  - Objects can now be moved to areas without detected planes
  - Uses camera's view matrix to project movement

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ar): improve drag drop to work on all screen positions

Root cause: Camera ray projection fallback was using absolute screen
coordinates instead of delta movement, causing erratic object positioning.

Solution:
- Track last screen position (dragLastScreenX/Y) for delta calculation
- Use screen delta movement instead of absolute normalized coordinates
- Project finger movement to world XZ plane using camera vectors
- Apply delta to current position instead of recalculating from scratch
- Scale pixel-to-world conversion based on object distance from camera

Also includes style improvements to TrashZone:
- Add semi-transparent background
- Add subtle shadow effect
- Add subtle border
- Center text properly
- Increase size for better text fit

Fixes: drag-everywhere-bug
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(ui): improve TrashZone design with transparency and centering

- Add semi-transparent background (alpha 0.7 normal, 0.85 hovered)
- Add subtle shadow effect for better visibility
- Add subtle border for definition
- Center text with TextAlign.Center and fillMaxWidth
- Increase size to 100x90dp for better text fit
- Add fillMaxSize to Column for proper centering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ui): correct trash zone detection bounds for drag-to-delete

Root cause: isOverTrashZone() was using incorrect dimensions (80dp x 80dp)
that didn't match the actual TrashZone component (100dp x 90dp).

Solution: Updated trash zone detection to use the correct dimensions:
- trashZoneWidth = 100.dp (matches component's width)
- trashZoneHeight = 90.dp (matches component's height)
- trashZonePadding = 16.dp

The detection area now correctly matches the visual trash zone position,
enabling reliable drag-to-delete functionality.

Fixes: trash-zone-delete-bug

* fix(gallery): lazy inject GalleryViewModel to prevent crash

Root cause: GalleryViewModel was being eagerly injected at App()
composition time, causing loadMedia() to run immediately even
before user navigates to Gallery screen. This triggered premature
MediaRepository access.

Solution: Move GalleryViewModel injection inside the Screen.Gallery
when branch, so it only initializes when user actually navigates
to the Gallery screen.

commit.message:
Fix: change global datalabels from false to {display:false}

'false' completely disables the plugin, ignoring all per-dataset configs.
'{display:false}' keeps the plugin active but hidden by default, allowing
per-dataset datalabels overrides to work (goals, seat util, rev bridge).
